Details: Fluoride removal from groundwater by conventional treatment (i.e., alum coagulation, flocculation, sedimentation and filtration) was found to be moderately effective in one study (between 50% and 71% removal achieved) in both batch and pilot scale experiments.  Alum dosages used were higher than what is typically used in drinking water treatment.  Percent removals with and without granular activated carbon at the pilot scale were not significantly different.
